p.strong: POSIX rename() guarantees atomic visibility of filesystem changes

div.p: The rename(oldpath, newpath) system call is atomic:

ol.li: Visibility: Processes see either the old file or the new file, never both or neither.
Replacement: If newpath exists, it is atomicaly replaced.
Recovery: If the system crashes during the rename, the filesystem will recover to a state where either the old path or the new path exists.

g.title: Write new data to a hidden temporary file on the same partition
Flush kernel buffers to physical media
Atomic system call: old file is replaced or new is created
Write new data to a hidden temporary file on the same partition
Flush kernel buffers to physical media
Atomic system call: old file is replaced or new is created
